When facing an alkane gas flame, first carefully inspect the source of the flame and the surrounding environment to assess risks. To extinguish the fire, two approaches can be considered: one is to shut off relevant valves to cut off the gas supply and reduce fuel input, and the other is to use water spray to cool the flame and suppress it. Finally, monitor the fire to track its development and ensure it is fully extinguished.

For high-temperature liquid flames, begin by inspecting the scene to assess the extent and potential spread of the fire. Use an asbestos felt to cover the flames and smother them, effectively cutting off the oxygen supply. Then, spray water to cool down the temperature. Finally monitor the fire to ensure it is fully extinguished and that there are no residual hazards.

For high-voltage electrical flames, carefully inspection is the highest priority. Then, one good way to extinguish it is operating switches to cut off the power supply, reducing the risk of electric shock and fire spread. Another way to extinguish the flame is using foam for liquid spray to extinguish the fire and use mental net to prevent leakage of electricity. Throughout the process, repeatedly monitor to ensure safety and complete extinguishment.

When rescuing trapped persons, first inspect the scene to locate and assess the condition of the victim. Then, clean up debris or hazards to create safe conditions for rescue. Finally, carefully rescure the person. Finally monitor the victim's condition and provide support until they are safely rescued.

When dealing with poisoned individuals, first inspect the scene to identify the type of toxin and the condition of the affected person. Then spray oxygen to them to avoid asphyxiation. Then carefully rescue them. Finally, monitor their recovery.

In the event of hydrogen sulfide leakage, immediately inspect the range of spread. If conditions allow, safely ignite the gas to control the hazard, then use solid sprays such as activated carbon for adsorption. Continue monitoring the site to ensure residual hazards are minimized.

For storage tank protection, fitst carefully check the status of the tank to get key infomation. Then apply water to cool and shield the tank, then perform repair as needed and monitor for damage or leaks.
